channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Adebayo Adelabu resigns as Nigeria’s Power Minister to pursue Oyo governorship bid amid ongoing power crisis
item.description: Adebayo Adelabu, wey serve as Nigeria Minister of Power for about three years, don resign from him position on April 22, 2026. Him submit resignation letter to President Bola Tinubu, thanking am for t...

item.title: U.S. administration lowers federal classification of state-licensed medical marijuana
item.description: In a significant policy change, former U.S. President Donald Trump announced the federal reclassification of medical marijuana that is legally licensed at the state level. This shift downgrades the dr...

item.title: INEC to display voters register nationwide from April 29 to May 5 for claims and objections
item.description: Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don finish the second phase of its Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) exercise nationwide, wit total of 3,748,704 completed registrations recorded acr...

item.title: Violence Erupts in Durban During Local Protest Against Immigrants
item.description: In Durban, South Africa, protests against immigrants turned violent earlier this week, leading to the closure of many businesses owned by foreign nationals. The protest, which started as a mass cleanu...

item.title: Kenya and Uganda Plan Regional Oil Refinery in Tanzania
item.description: Kenya and Uganda are exploring plans to establish a joint oil refinery in Tanga, Tanzania, with the aim of tapping the East African region's crude oil resources and boosting energy security. President...

item.title: Air Transat Reduces Flights Amid Rising Jet Fuel Costs
item.description: Air Transat has announced a reduction in its flight schedule due to soaring jet fuel prices triggered by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The airline will suspend six per cent of its flights b...

item.title: Creamline wins 11th PVL title after defeating Cignal in All-Filipino finals
item.description: Nagbalik sa tuktok ang Creamline Cool Smashers matapos makuha ang kanilang ika-11 kampeonato sa Premier Volleyball League (PVL) sa pamamagitan ng panalo kontra Cignal Super Spikers sa 2026 All-Filipin...

item.title: Concerns over foreign political campaigns influencing Canadian unions
item.description: The article examines the growing influence of Labour for Palestine Canada (L4P), a network of union activists advocating for international political causes within Canadian unions. Originating from a h...

item.title: Eskom’s Internal Investigation into Diesel Procurement Nears Completion
item.description: Eskom, the South African state-owned power utility, has announced that its internal investigation into potential irregularities in diesel procurement and storage contracts is in its final stages. The ...

item.title: FBI arrests dozens in major Southern California operation targeting Mexican Mafia
item.description: In a coordinated operation early Thursday, FBI agents arrested at least two dozen Mexican Mafia members and associates in Southern California, as part of a larger investigation spanning two years. The...

item.title: CBSE to conduct Class 10 second board exams from May 15 to 21 under new improvement policy
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has officially released the schedule for the Class 10 second board examinations for 2026, which will be conducted from May 15 to May 21. The examination...

item.title: PCSO reports no winners in April 23, 2026 Lotto 6/42 and Superlotto 6/49 draws
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no bettor successfully matched the winning combinations in the major lotto draws held on Thursday night, April 23, 2026. As a result, bo...

item.title: Nigeria spends over $400 million annually on tomato paste imports despite local production
item.description: The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) has revealed that Nigeria spends over $400 million annually on tomato paste imports, despite being one of the world’s largest producers of fresh tomatoes. ...

item.title: Paramount Receives Shareholder Approval to Acquire Warner Bros
item.description: Paramount has received overwhelming approval from shareholders for its acquisition of Warner Bros, signaling a significant consolidation in the entertainment industry. The vote demonstrates strong inv...

item.title: NSC to Honor Tanzanian Athletes with Annual Awards on May 9 in Dar es Salaam
item.description: The National Sports Council (NSC) is set to host its annual awards ceremony on May 9, 2026, at The Super Dome in Dar es Salaam. This event, now in its fourth year, recognizes outstanding Tanzanian ath...

item.title: Africa’s Growing Domestic Capital Signals Shift in Development Financing Strategy
item.description: Africa is now increasingly depending on its own financial resources to support development, according to the State of Africa’s Infrastructure Report 2026 by the Africa Finance Corporation (AFC). The r...

item.title: Museo del Galeón highlights the Philippines’ maritime history and Manila galleon heritage
item.description: The Museo del Galeón shines a spotlight on the Philippines’ deep and enduring maritime heritage, particularly its role in the historic Manila-Acapulco galleon trade. The museum serves as a cultural an...

item.title: Ogbakor Etche IAUE Chapter Shows Solidarity with Rivers Education Commissioner
item.description: The Honourable Commissioner for Education in Rivers State, Dr. Peters Nwagor, welcomed members of the Ogbakor Etche IAUE Rumuolumeni Chapter for a courtesy visit. The group, led by Chapter Chairman Pr...

item.title: Commission finds October 29 election violence in Tanzania was organised and funded operation
item.description: Tanzania’s Commission of Inquiry into the October 29, 2025 post-election violence has concluded that the incidents were not spontaneous or peaceful demonstrations, but rather a well-organised operatio...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Orders Inquiry Over Non-Functional Weather Warning Systems in GB
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has expressed strong concern over the non-functional early weather warning systems in Gilgit-Baltistan, which are crucial for protection against climate hazards such as g...
